goal: Add a side-effect-free live AICOS fetch/cache readiness contract that validates endpoint references, auth references, cache policy, and quarantine policy before any later live AICOS connector may fetch card snapshots. Do not call AICOS, create a cache, add persistence, add routes, change packages, deploy, or modify frontend/runtime behavior.
scope_out: Do not call AICOS, maya-core, providers, GitHub, database, runtime dispatch, or external services.
Do not add cache persistence, package scripts, dependencies, environment variables, deployment notes, routes, or frontend files.
Do not change BPK-010 intake behavior.
claims: Fetch/cache readiness can be validated without making a live AICOS request.
Endpoint, auth, TTL, stale behavior, and quarantine policy must be explicit before live fetch.
Unsafe cache policies block live fetch readiness.
The contract is side-effect-free and tested independently.
assumptions: A later connector block may perform the actual fetch.
BPK-010 remains the intake normalizer for fetched snapshots.
Auth references are opaque names and must not contain token values.
dod: builder/src/liveAicosFetchCacheContract.ts exports side-effect-free fetch/cache readiness types and classifier.
The classifier returns ready, review_required, or blocked with deterministic blockers.
The classifier validates endpointRef, authRef, TTL bounds, stale behavior, and quarantine policy.
builder/tests/liveAicosFetchCacheContract.test.ts covers ready, disabled, missing auth, unsafe token-like auth, TTL bounds, missing quarantine, and no-mutation behavior.
review-packets/BPK-015.md records scope, safety invariants, gates, and verification.
The focused fetch/cache contract test and npm run typecheck in builder/ pass.
stop_conditions: A live AICOS call, cache persistence, route, provider call, DB write, GitHub write, deployment change, package change, or frontend file becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-010 created a side-effect-free card snapshot intake normalizer. BPK-015 should define fetch/cache readiness without performing live fetch or persistence.
